headline: Micron plans second chip facility at newly acquired Taiwan site
summary: U.S. memory chipmaker Micron ‌Technology said on Monday ‌it plans to build a ​second manufacturing facility in Taiwan at the Tongluo site it recently acquired from ‌Powerchip ⁠Semiconductor Manufacturing Corp. The new facility will help ⁠it expand supply of leading-edge DRAM products including ​high-bandwidth memory (HBM) ​to ​support surging AI ‌demand, the company said. Micron also said it has completed the acquisition of PSMC's Tongluo P5 site and ‌the new ​second facility would ​be ​of similar scale ‌to the existing fab ​in ​Miaoli County.

content.title: Micron plans second chip facility at newly acquired Taiwan site
content.summary: U.S. memory chipmaker Micron ‌Technology said on Monday ‌it plans to build a ​second manufacturing facility in Taiwan at the Tongluo site it recently acquired from ‌Powerchip ⁠Semiconductor Manufacturing Corp. The new facility will help ⁠it expand supply of leading-edge DRAM products including ​high-bandwidth memory (HBM) ​to ​support surging AI ‌demand, the company said. Micron also said it has completed the acquisition of PSMC's Tongluo P5 site and ‌the new ​second facility would ​be ​of similar scale ‌to the existing fab ​in ​Miaoli County.

headline: Micron Taiwan Fab Deal Ties AI DRAM Growth To Rich Valuation
summary: Micron Technology completed its acquisition of Powerchip Semiconductor Manufacturing Corporation’s P5 fab site in Taiwan. The facility is intended to support advanced DRAM production aimed at growing AI memory demand. Micron plans to retrofit existing cleanroom space immediately and pursue additional construction by the end of fiscal 2026. For investors watching Micron Technology (NasdaqGS: MU), this move comes as the stock trades around $426.13, with very large 1 year and 3 year returns...

The memory-chip maker said it plans to build a second manufacturing facility at its site in Taiwan by the end of 2026.

headline: Stocks to Watch Monday Recap: Nvidia, Meta, Dollar Tree
summary: ↗️ Meta (META), Nebius (NBIS): Shares in the Facebook parent rose 2.3% after Reuters reported that the company is planning sweeping layoffs. Meta also inked a $27 billion deal to get AI infrastructure capacity from Nebius, whose shares rallied 15%.
